scope.summary:
Goal: Implement a complete Monaco/VS Code parity keymap for the native code editor, with configurable keybindings stored in the operator's shell settings file. This is the unifying MT that consolidates all the per-MT pre-wired key handlers (Ctrl+D from MT-003, Ctrl+F/H from MT-004, Ctrl+Shift+[/] from MT-005, Ctrl+G from MT-006) into a single deterministic dispatch table — the canonical reference for 'what does this key do in the code editor'. The keymap is also exposed to swarm agents via AccessKit command nodes (reuse accessibility/* from WP-011) and via MCP tools (reuse mcp/* from WP-011) so agents can dispatch editor commands without simulating keystrokes. Expected diff shape: new `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs` (the binding table and resolver), new `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap_settings.rs` (settings file loader/writer), edits to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` (replace all ad-hoc key handlers with the single dispatcher).

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs` — ports `editor_keymap.ts` to Rust. Defines `KeyChord { key: egui::Key, modifiers: egui::Modifiers }`. `KeyBinding { chord: KeyChord, action: CodeEditorAction, description: &'static str }`. `CodeEditorAction` enum — complete VS Code parity set: `MoveCursorLeft`, `MoveCursorRight`, `MoveCursorUp`, `MoveCursorDown`, `MoveCursorWordLeft`, `MoveCursorWordRight`, `MoveCursorLineStart`, `MoveCursorLineEnd`, `MoveCursorDocStart`, `MoveCursorDocEnd`, `SelectLeft`, `SelectRight`, `SelectUp`, `SelectDown`, `SelectWordLeft`, `SelectWordRight`, `SelectLineStart`, `SelectLineEnd`, `SelectAll`, `DeleteLeft`, `DeleteRight`, `DeleteWordLeft`, `DeleteWordRight`, `DeleteLine`, `InsertNewline`, `InsertTab`, `IndentLine`, `DedentLine`, `ToggleComment`, `DuplicateLine`, `MoveLineUp`, `MoveLineDown`, `AddCursorAbove`, `AddCursorBelow`, `SelectNextOccurrence`, `CancelMultiCursor`, `OpenFind`, `OpenReplace`, `FindNext`, `FindPrev`, `CloseFind`, `FoldAtCursor`, `UnfoldAtCursor`, `FoldAll`, `UnfoldAll`, `GoToLine`, `GoToDefinition`, `ShowReferences`, `ShowHover`, `TriggerCompletion`, `AcceptCompletion`, `DismissCompletion`, `Undo`, `Redo`, `Save`, `OpenCommandPalette`. `Keymap { bindings: Vec<KeyBinding> }`. `Keymap::default_vscode()` returns the canonical Monaco-parity binding table. `Keymap::resolve(chord: KeyChord) -> Option<CodeEditorAction>` — O(N) lookup (N is small, <60 bindings). `Keymap::from_settings(settings: &KeymapSettings) -> Keymap` — merges user overrides over the default table.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap_settings.rs` — `KeymapSettings { overrides: Vec<KeymapOverride> }` where `KeymapOverride { action: String, chord: String }`. `KeymapSettings::load_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, Error>` reads a JSON file (the shell settings file that already exists for the operator). `KeymapSettings::save_to_file(path: &Path, settings: &Self) -> Result<(), Error>` writes back. The settings file path is `~/.handshake/keymap.json` (use `dirs::home_dir()` for portability — no hardcoded paths per GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-004). `KeymapSettings::chord_from_str(s: &str) -> Result<KeyChord, Error>` parses human-readable chords like `"Ctrl+Shift+P"`, `"Mod+F"`, `"Alt+Up"` into `KeyChord`. Mod = Ctrl on Windows/Linux, Command on macOS (detect via `cfg!(target_os = "macos")`).
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — remove all ad-hoc `egui::Event::Key` handlers added in MT-003, MT-004, MT-005, MT-006. Replace with a single `process_keymap(input: &egui::InputState, keymap: &Keymap) -> Vec<CodeEditorAction>` call, then dispatch each action to the appropriate handler method. This is the single key dispatch point.

Default binding table (canonical Monaco/VS Code parity — key subset, full table in implementation):
| Chord                | Action                  |
|----------------------|-------------------------|
| Ctrl+F               | OpenFind                |
| Ctrl+H               | OpenReplace             |
| Ctrl+G               | GoToLine                |
| Ctrl+D               | SelectNextOccurrence    |
| Ctrl+Alt+Up          | AddCursorAbove          |
| Ctrl+Alt+Down        | AddCursorBelow          |
| Escape               | CancelMultiCursor / CloseFind / DismissCompletion (context-sensitive) |
| Ctrl+Shift+[         | FoldAtCursor            |
| Ctrl+Shift+]         | UnfoldAtCursor          |
| Ctrl+K Ctrl+0        | FoldAll (two-chord)     |
| Ctrl+K Ctrl+J        | UnfoldAll (two-chord)   |
| Ctrl+Shift+P         | OpenCommandPalette      |
| F12                  | GoToDefinition          |
| Shift+F12            | ShowReferences          |
| Ctrl+Space           | TriggerCompletion       |
| Tab                  | AcceptCompletion (when popup open) / InsertTab |
| Ctrl+Z               | Undo                    |
| Ctrl+Y / Ctrl+Shift+Z| Redo                    |
| Ctrl+S               | Save                    |
| Ctrl+/               | ToggleComment           |
| Alt+Up/Down          | MoveLineUp / MoveLineDown |
| Ctrl+Shift+K         | DeleteLine              |
| Ctrl+D               | DuplicateLine (when no selection) |

Note on two-chord bindings: maintain `pending_chord: Option<KeyChord>` in `CodeEditorPanel`; if a binding has a prefix chord (e.g. Ctrl+K), set pending_chord after the first chord and resolve the action after the second chord.

AccessKit command surface: for each `CodeEditorAction` variant, add an `author_id = "code_editor_cmd_{action_name}"` `Role::Button` node in the AccessKit tree with no visual representation (hidden from the UI but accessible to agents), reusing the WP-011 accessibility integration (accessibility/mod.rs, accessibility/live.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/snapshot.rs). Agents can trigger commands by activating these nodes. The same actions are also exposed to swarm models through the WP-011 MCP surface (mcp/tools.rs, mcp/action.rs) so swarm models can dispatch editor commands without knowing key bindings.

Configurable keybindings: when the operator changes a binding in `~/.handshake/keymap.json`, the next frame reloads the keymap (watch the file for changes using `notify` crate or poll on a timer). The UI panel header has a 'Configure keybindings' button that opens the keymap settings file in the system editor (via `open::that()` crate) or in a built-in key binding editor panel (future MT; for now, just open the file).

React reference files:
- `app/src/lib/editor/editor_keymap.ts` — direct porting reference. Port: `KeyBinding` interface, `EDITOR_KEY_BINDINGS` table, `resolveShortcut`, `chordFromEvent`, `CODE_BLOCK_GLOBAL_ACTIONS`, `isGlobalEditorAction`, `shouldContainChordFromCodeBlock`, `bindingsForAction`. The native editor's `Keymap::resolve` is the equivalent of `resolveShortcut`.
- `app/src/lib/editor/editor_commands.ts` — the full command catalog for the rich editor; the code editor has its own `CodeEditorAction` enum but follows the same design principle (command ids as the single source of truth).
- `app/src/lib/editor/editor_command_palette_request.ts` — the command palette request type; the native equivalent uses `OpenCommandPalette` action to open the palette.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Implement `Keymap::default_vscode()` with all bindings in a `vec![KeyBinding { ... }, ...]` literal. Keep the list sorted by chord string for documentation purposes.
2. Two-chord handling: use `pending_chord: Option<KeyChord>`. In `process_keymap`, if a key event matches a prefix chord (Ctrl+K), set pending; if pending is set and the next key event matches the second chord, resolve the two-chord action and clear pending. Clear pending after 3 seconds without a second chord.
3. Context-sensitive Escape: check current editor state — if completion popup is open, `Escape` = `DismissCompletion`; if find bar is open, `Escape` = `CloseFind`; if multi-cursor, `Escape` = `CancelMultiCursor`; otherwise `Escape` = no-op. This replaces the fragmented Escape handlers from MT-003, MT-004.
4. `chord_from_str`: parse `"Ctrl+Shift+P"` by splitting on `+`, mapping modifier strings to `egui::Modifiers` flags, mapping the key string to `egui::Key` using a lookup table.
5. AccessKit command buttons: in `CodeEditorPanel::show()`, after the main editor UI, call `ui.ctx().with_accessibility_parent(panel_node, |ui| { for action in CodeEditorAction::all() { let resp = ui.add_invisible(egui::Button::new(action.name())); resp.id // set author_id } })` — use the AccessKit integration API to emit hidden buttons.
6. File watcher: use `std::time::SystemTime` to poll `keymap.json` mtime every 5 seconds; reload on change. Avoid the `notify` crate if not already in the dependency tree (adds complexity). Document in a comment.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 (CodeEditorPanel exists), MT-003 (CursorSet for multi-cursor actions), MT-004 (FindState for find/replace actions), MT-005 (FoldSet for fold/unfold actions). All ad-hoc key handlers from MT-003/004/005/006 must be replaced — coordinate with the coder to remove them when this MT lands.
Risk if missed: Fragmented key handlers from prior MTs are not unified — behavior is inconsistent, hard to reconfigure, and not accessible to swarm agents.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_resolve` passes — tests: Ctrl+F -> OpenFind, Ctrl+H -> OpenReplace, Ctrl+G -> GoToLine, Ctrl+D -> SelectNextOccurrence, F12 -> GoToDefinition, Ctrl+S -> Save, Escape (find open) -> CloseFind, Escape (no state) -> None.
AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_two_chord` passes — tests: Ctrl+K then Ctrl+0 -> FoldAll; Ctrl+K then Ctrl+J -> UnfoldAll; Ctrl+K then 3-second timeout -> pending cleared, no action.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_chord_parse` passes — tests: 'Ctrl+Shift+P' parses to KeyChord with ctrl+shift and key=P; 'Alt+Up' parses to alt+Up; invalid string returns Err.

AC-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_override` passes — test: load a KeymapSettings JSON that remaps Ctrl+F to GoToLine; Keymap::from_settings resolves Ctrl+F -> GoToLine instead of OpenFind.
AC-005: egui_kittest AccessKit dump contains at least 5 button nodes with author_id pattern 'code_editor_cmd_*' (OpenFind, OpenReplace, GoToLine, GoToDefinition, Save).

AC-006: The per-feature ad-hoc key handlers from MT-003/004/005/006/008 are CONSOLIDATED into a SINGLE keymap dispatcher (process_keymap -> Keymap::resolve -> dispatch). NOTE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): those handlers live in panel.rs (CodeEditorPanel::process_cursor_input), NOT mod.rs - the contract's mod.rs assumption is wrong (editor input state is on CodeEditorPanel in panel.rs, as MT-008 found). Verify NO scattered per-feature egui::Event::Key match arms remain outside the single dispatcher in panel.rs; egui::Event::Key/Text may appear ONLY inside the unified process_keymap + the live-typing handler. Proof = targeted rg over panel.rs + a test asserting one dispatch path.
AC-007: `keymap.json` override file is loaded from `~/.handshake/keymap.json` — path constructed via `dirs::home_dir()` not a hardcoded path string.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_resolve` — GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_two_chord` — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_chord_parse` — GREEN, logged.
PT-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native keymap_override` — GREEN, logged.
PT-005: egui_kittest test `keymap_accesskit_commands` — AccessKit JSON dump parsed for 'code_editor_cmd_' nodes, at least 5 present.

PT-006: Verify consolidated dispatch in panel.rs (the real input-handler file): rg -n 'egui::Event::Key' src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs shows key handling ONLY inside the unified process_keymap dispatcher + the live-typing (Event::Text/Backspace) handler, not scattered per-feature arms. Logged.
implementation_notes: macOS Mod key: `cfg!(target_os = "macos")` -> use `egui::Modifiers::MAC_CMD`; otherwise use `egui::Modifiers::CTRL`. Encode this as a const `fn mod_key() -> egui::Modifiers` to keep the binding table OS-agnostic.

Do not use `egui::Key` string names for settings parsing — maintain an explicit `key_from_str` lookup table covering all keys in the binding table plus common extras (F1-F12, arrows, page up/down, home, end, escape, tab, enter, backspace, delete).
`dirs` crate (MIT) — minimal dependency for home directory resolution. Already likely in the workspace; if not, add it.

AccessKit hidden buttons: use `ui.skip_ahead_auto_ids(n)` or set stable `egui::Id::new(format!("code_editor_cmd_{}", action.name()))` explicitly. Hidden = no visual area, but the AccessKit node is emitted. Verify this pattern works with the egui version in use.

Command palette integration: `OpenCommandPalette` action should dispatch to the same command palette used by the rest of the shell — reuse the WP-011 command palette (command_palette.rs) backed by command_registry.rs; do not build a second palette. Wire via an `mpsc::Sender<ShellCommand>` passed to `CodeEditorPanel::new()`.

Cross-WP dependency (prose, not a depends_on id): this MT consolidates and replaces the per-MT key handlers and integrates with WP-011 shell primitives. The reused shell modules — command_palette.rs + command_registry.rs (palette/commands), event_bus.rs (cross-pane dispatch), accessibility/* (AccessKit nodes), and mcp/* (swarm command surface) — all ship in WP-KERNEL-011 and must already be present in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ before this MT lands.

ToggleComment implementation: for the primary cursor's line, detect if the line starts with the language's comment prefix (e.g. `//` for Rust/JS, `#` for Python); toggle the prefix. Support multi-cursor: apply to all cursor lines.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-003 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): MT-010 MUST wire the multi-cursor commands (Alt+Click add-cursor, Ctrl+D select-next-occurrence, Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down add-cursor-above/below) through the existing command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs + command_palette.rs so they are discoverable and rebindable (MT-003 implemented them as a direct egui input handler only). The handlers already exist in code_editor/mod.rs process_cursor_input; MT-010 routes them through the command bus + keymap table.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-003 (step 7 live typing): MT-010 MUST wire live keyboard text input at all cursors - egui::Event::Text -> CursorSet::insert_at_all, Key::Backspace/Delete -> delete_at_all (and buffer_version bump for highlight-cache invalidation per MT-002). The CursorSet model methods are implemented + AC-006-tested in MT-003; MT-010 connects the keyboard events to them. This is the core editor typing loop.

FILE LOCATION CORRECTION (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): the ad-hoc key handlers from MT-003/004/005/006/008 live in panel.rs (CodeEditorPanel::process_cursor_input + pump_code_intelligence), NOT mod.rs. The unified keymap dispatcher, the carried-forward live-typing (Event::Text -> insert_at_all, Backspace/Delete -> delete_at_all + buffer_version bump), and the command-bus routing all wire into panel.rs. panel.rs is in allowed_paths. mod.rs gets only module decls + the open_* factory/dispatch glue.

KEYBINDING PERSISTENCE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): the contract names ~/.handshake/keymap.json AND 'the shell settings file that already exists'. Prefer the EXISTING PostgreSQL-backed settings surface (/workspaces/:id/settings via SettingsClient, the same surface MT-072 uses for editor settings) to avoid a SECOND settings authority. A user-editable file is acceptable for VS Code-parity ONLY if portable (dirs::home_dir(), no hardcoded path, NO SQLite) and reconciled with MT-072 so there is one authority. Do NOT introduce SQLite or a hardcoded path. Coordinate the persistence home with MT-072.

ACCESSKIT COMMAND NODES (RISK-002): cache the code_editor_cmd_* hidden command nodes OUTSIDE the render loop; rebuild only when the keymap changes (not 50+ nodes every frame). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: use field-correct nearest accesskit 0.21.1 Role if Button/etc. differ; ACs assert author_id. The live-typing wiring + popup-key consolidation must remain non-focus-stealing (HBR-QUIET).
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/editor_keymap.ts — KeyBinding interface, EDITOR_KEY_BINDINGS table, resolveShortcut, chordFromEvent, CODE_BLOCK_GLOBAL_ACTIONS, shouldContainChordFromCodeBlock, bindingsForAction — all ported directly
app/src/lib/editor/editor_commands.ts — command catalog design pattern (command id as single source of truth)
app/src/lib/editor/editor_command_palette_request.ts — EditorCommandPaletteRequest type; OpenCommandPalette action triggers equivalent
binds_backend_api: None — keymap is purely in-process. Settings file at ~/.handshake/keymap.json is a local file read/write only.

risks: RISK-001: Two-chord pending state is never cleared if the user presses an unrecognized second chord — the editor stops responding to single-chord shortcuts. Mitigate: clear pending on any key event that is not a known second chord for the pending prefix.

RISK-002: AccessKit hidden button nodes accumulate per CodeEditorAction every frame — if there are 50 actions and the tree is rebuilt every frame, this is 50 extra nodes per frame. Mitigate: cache the AccessKit command nodes outside the render loop; rebuild only when keymap changes.

RISK-003: `chord_from_str` silently ignores unknown key names and returns Ok with a wrong chord. Mitigate: `key_from_str` returns `Err` for unknown keys; `chord_from_str` propagates the error; invalid settings file entries are skipped with a warning log.

RISK-004: macOS Mod key detection at compile time via `cfg!(target_os)` means the binary is not cross-platform-overridable at runtime. This is acceptable — Handshake is native and platform-specific binaries are expected.

RISK-005: Removing ad-hoc key handlers from MT-003/004/005/006 during this MT may conflict with in-flight coder sessions on those MTs if they are not yet complete. Mitigate: MT-010 depends_on MT-003, MT-004, MT-005 — those must be DONE before MT-010 starts.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Two-chord pending-clear-on-unknown is required — unit test in AC-002 covers the timeout case; add a test for 'wrong second chord clears pending'.
MC-002: `key_from_str` Err on unknown key — verified in AC-003 'invalid string returns Err' test.
MC-003: `depends_on` ordering enforces MT-003/004/005 completion before MT-010 starts — do not relax this constraint.
MC-004: AccessKit command node caching — document the caching strategy in a code comment.

kernel_builder_carried_forward.note: Full MT-010 contract integration of these happens when MT-010 is activated; recorded now for preservation (GLOBAL-PRESERVATION).

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-003 carry-forward + MT-008 mod.rs-vs-panel.rs finding + MT-072
corrections: handlers + live-typing + dispatcher live in panel.rs not mod.rs (added to allowed_paths)
AC-006/PT-006 target panel.rs + softened from literal 0-matches to single-dispatcher consolidation
keybinding persistence prefers the PG settings surface; portable file only if reconciled with MT-072, no SQLite
cache AccessKit command nodes; accesskit variant fallback

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_90da0b44 (impl + adversarial-review + harden)
commits: 8bb2c204 feat MT-010 Monaco-parity keymap + single key dispatcher
ff0a3b50 fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..007 + PT-001..006 GREEN; 497 lib + test_keymap 12 + all regression bins green; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; theme guard green; single egui::Event::Key dispatch site in panel.rs (AC-006); 56 code_editor_cmd_* AccessKit nodes; live typing (Event::Text->insert_text) wired; portable ~/.handshake/keymap.json via dirs (no hardcoded path, no SQLite); Keybindings UI toggle visually verified; no leakage.
implementation_result.bugs_caught_and_fixed: Review caught 3 real must-fixes, harden fixed all: (1) forward-Delete at EOF deleted BACKWARD (silent data corruption) -> delete_forward_at_all with EOF no-op + 5 tests; (2) GoToDefinition/ShowReferences/ShowHover dispatched to an EMPTY request_code_nav seam (anti-vibe Sub-rule 1) -> rewired to the REAL MT-008 trigger_hover/lookup_symbols/get_references (Sub-rule 2 satisfied), deleted the seam; (3) process_keymap/resolve_contextual had ZERO runtime coverage + AC-001 Escape was a tautology -> added 4 real-key-event egui_kittest tests through the live loop (Escape->CloseFind, goto-line Enter->submit-not-newline, two-chord FoldAll + deterministic timeout-clear).
implementation_result.carried_forward: ShowReferences references-panel UI (backend round-trip wired + observable via last_references(), but no rendered panel) + live F12/Shift+F12 caret-jump+references-count proof (needs bound workspace+runtime) -> E11/host MT (MT-069) + WP_VALIDATOR with backend. Save/Undo/Redo/OpenCommandPalette route to send_to_command_bus (no-op without a host Sender) -> E11 host-mount wires the Sender (MT-069).
deferred: default keymap omits some VS Code chords (Ctrl+D->DuplicateLine when no-selection, FindNext/Prev, Indent/Dedent, ShowHover chord) - dispatchable via command-node/MCP but no keyboard chord; expand default_vscode() in a follow-up (operator-scoped)
line-edit ops (delete_line/indent/dedent/duplicate/move_line) act on the primary cursor line only (toggle_comment iterates all); multi-cursor whole-line is a later refinement
keymap.json hot-reload polls mtime every 5s (1s granularity); pre-existing design
pre-existing accesskit_consumer 0.30.1/0.31.0 dup (egui_kittest vs accesskit_winit); core single 0.21.1; WP-level hygiene note
